task_text: Meridian Group has asked for a product demo of Helios in two weeks. Based on what you can find in the sandbox, write a short assessment (3-4 paragraphs) of whether we are ready to demo and what, if anything, needs to be resolved first. Save it as demo_readiness_assessment.txt.

content.text: I’ve found your sandbox files and notes on the Helios demo request. I’ll review the project data and draft your readiness assessment.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===

TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS

TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS

Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — REST API Reference (Internal)
Last updated: 2026-07-05 | Base URL: https://api.helios.novabridge.internal/v1
Authentication: Bearer token (OAuth 2.0) — Header: Authorization: Bearer <token>

Endpoint                        Method  Description                              Status
------------------------------  ------  ---------------------------------------  -------
/health                         GET     System health check                      Stable
/ingest/batch                   POST    Submit batch data file (CSV/JSON/XML)    Stable
/ingest/stream                  POST    Open streaming ingestion session         Stable
/ingest/status/{job_id}         GET     Check ingestion job status               Stable
/pipeline/runs                  GET     List recent pipeline executions           Stable
/pipeline/runs/{run_id}         GET     Pipeline run details and metrics          Stable
/transform/preview              POST    Preview transformation output (dry-run)   Stable
/dashboard/summary              GET     Aggregate pipeline metrics for dashboard  Stable
/dashboard/alerts               GET     Active pipeline alerts                    Stable
/ingest/jobs/{job_id}           DELETE  Cancel in-progress ingestion job          Stable
/config/mappings                GET     List active transformation mapping rules  Beta
/config/mappings/{id}           PUT     Update a mapping rule                     Beta

Rate limits: 1,000 req/min per authenticated client. HTTP 429 returned when exceeded.
Support contact: api-support@novabridge.com

content.text: I’ve gathered the current status, test results, and risks for Helios. The system isn’t demo-ready yet due to critical blockers, so I’ll draft the assessment outlining what needs to be resolved before the Meridian demo.

input.content:
Product Demo Readiness Assessment — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Demo Date: July 25, 2026 (2 weeks) | Assessment Date: July 11, 2026 equivalent

Current Status: NOT YET READY, but achievable within the two-week window if critical blockers are cleared. Per the July 6 status report, Helios is ON TRACK with minor risks: API Gateway is 100% complete and stable, Data Ingestion is 85% complete, Client Dashboard is 60% complete, and Integration Testing is 40% complete. The core architecture (ingestion layer, Spark transformation engine, API gateway, dashboard, Postgres + S3 store) is implemented and auth flows have passed QA. However, end-to-end functionality required for the demo is not yet proven.

Three high-severity blockers directly threaten the demo requirements defined on July 8 (live data ingestion, end-to-end pipeline walkthrough, dashboard with real data per contract Section 9.3). First, HEL-D-014: date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY format, which affects ~40% of Meridian source data. This is listed as R-01 High/High risk and is blocking Task HEL-243. It is dependent on clean sample data from Meridian IT due July 15. Since Meridian sponsor explicitly requires live ingestion, not mock data, we cannot demo successfully without this fix. Second, HEL-D-015: large file ingestion crashes with memory overflow at ~210k records, failing QA TC-507. This caps our demo dataset and contradicts the 500k/min throughput design target. Third, GeoSync v2.1 mapping library license renewal (R-02, Task HEL-248) is pending legal review due July 14. Without renewal, we cannot legally show map features in the dashboard.

Additional gaps increase demo risk. The dashboard test suite is only 1/4 complete — chart rendering (HEL-242) is still in progress and load-time benchmarking (HEL-247, R-03) has not started, despite client concerns about performance on large datasets. Q2 deliverables show three OVERDUE items: integration test suite setup (rescheduled to July 20), UAT environment provisioning blocked by Meridian IT access (Task HEL-246 At Risk, awaiting infrastructure diagram due July 12 and access by July 19), and performance benchmarking report. Risk register also flags UAT timeline slippage (R-04) with its one-week buffer already consumed, and key-person dependency on Tech Lead Derek Okafor (R-05).

Recommendation: Proceed with demo preparation but treat the next 7 days as critical path. 1) Secure GeoSync license by July 14 and confirm Meridian sample data delivery by July 15 to unblock Amir Hassan. 2) Fix or workaround HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015 — if large-file fix cannot be completed, strictly limit demo ingest to <200k ISO-cleaned records and disclose limitation internally. 3) Complete dashboard chart modules and execute TC-602 through TC-604 with a <2 second load target for 100k records before July 22. 4) Prepare fallback demo agenda and send to Meridian by July 18 as committed, with clear scope on live data assumptions.

content.text:
Assessment completed based on project status, testing logs, architecture, risks, deliverables, meeting notes, sprint tasks, and Q3 roadmap.

**demo_readiness_assessment.txt** has been saved. It concludes:

- **Status: NOT YET READY but achievable** - API gateway is stable but ingestion (85%) and dashboard (60%) are incomplete and integration testing is only 40%.
- **Critical blockers:** HEL-D-014 date normalization failure (40% of Meridian data), HEL-D-015 memory overflow crash >200k records, and pending GeoSync license renewal due 7/14 — all violate the requirement for live data ingestion on 7/25.
- **Additional gaps:** Dashboard charts in-progress, performance benchmarking not started, 3 overdue Q2 deliverables, UAT environment at risk.
- **Recommendation:** Clear license and sample data dependencies by 7/14-7/15, fix/workaround ingestion defects, complete dashboard benchmarks before 7/22, and prepare fallback demo scope.
